checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfRevenue/CostOfGoodsSold line items are present in the provided data set for any period.

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Revenues=155872736.58 (2011) declining to Revenues=149118089.77 (2012), a -4.3% change
NetIncomeLoss=53147484.61 (2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=46110463.48 (2012), a smaller -13.2% decline despite rising InterestExpense=19629651.82 (2012) vs InterestExpense=21390487.56 (2011)
InterestExpense=20404706.14 (2008) growing to InterestExpense=35622575.17 (2014), while NetIncomeLoss margin stayed within a narrow 29.6%-34.1% of Revenues band across 2009-2014

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: DeferredRevenueCurrent=4973581.27 (2011-12-31) declined from DeferredRevenueCurrent=6871811.08 (2010-12-31) while Revenues rose from 140158394.48 (2010) to 155872736.58 (2011)
No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable or specific reserve line items are provided in the data set.

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2011-04-29; 10-K/A filed 2012-04-27; 10-K/A filed 2013-04-30; 10-K/A filed 2014-04-30; 10-K/A filed 2015-04-30 - a 10-K amendment in every single fiscal year from 2011 through 2015
10-Q/A filed 2011-08-30 (amending the 2011-06-30 quarter, used as the source for AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=27878353.85 and Goodwill=89365996.05 at 2011-06-30)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Assets, Liabilities, LongTermDebt, StockholdersEquity, and cash flow statement items span from 2008 through Q1 2015 (e.g., Revenues=91354838.53 (2008) through Revenues=40201253.83 (Q1 2015))
No InventoryNet, CostOfRevenue, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, or AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets concepts are provided, limiting analysis of CL4 and reducing precision on CL2/CL6

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition (or delayed customer collections/channel stuffing) causing receivables to build up faster than sales growth, particularly in the most recent periods where AR growth (30.8% in 2014) far outpaced revenue growth (6.6%), and where AR continued rising even as revenue declined 17.9% year-over-year in Q1 2015.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=38258349.87 (2013-12-31) to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=50057803.71 (2014-12-31), +30.8%, vs Revenues=182332054.07 (2013) to Revenues=194392809.53 (2014), +6.6%
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 and FY2014 balance sheets/income statements

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=52509402.92 (2015-03-31) up from 49647307.30 (2014-03-31) while Revenues fell from 48946850.39 (Q1 2014) to 40201253.83 (Q1 2015)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reclassification/restatement of operating cash flow between filings: FY2013 operating cash flow was originally reported at 98.1M in the 10-K filed 2014-02-27, but the same fiscal year figure (as continuing operations) was later reported at 85.6M in the 10-K filed 2015-03-02, a ~13% downward revision, suggesting either a discontinued-operations reclassification or a correction of previously overstated operating cash flow.

Possible aggressive capitalization/insufficient impairment of acquired intangible/royalty assets funded by rapidly increasing debt; intangibles grew 14.1% in 2012 even as revenue declined 4.3%, and intangibles constitute roughly 70% of total assets with minimal offsetting amortization expense visible in the data (only small 'Depreciation' amounts reported), raising concern about carrying values relative to underlying cash-generating performance.

rationale_evidence.quote: LongTermDebt=266944467.96 (2011-12-31) to LongTermDebt=384247400.00 (2012-12-31), a 44% increase funding acquisitions

top_signals: A 10-K/A amendment was filed in every fiscal year from 2011 through 2015, alongside a 10-Q/A in 2011, indicating chronic restatement/correction activity
Persistent, near-annual SEC comment-letter correspondence (UPLOAD/CORRESP filings) from 2010 through 2015 suggests sustained regulatory scrutiny of the company's disclosures
Accounts receivable grew 30.8% in 2014 versus only 6.6% revenue growth, and receivables continued rising in Q1 2015 even as revenue fell ~18% year-over-year
Operating cash flow for FY2013 was restated downward by roughly $12.5M (from 98.1M to 85.6M) between the original 10-K and a subsequent 10-K's comparative figures

Intangible assets (which constitute ~70% of total assets) grew disproportionately faster than revenue in at least one period (2012), funded by a sharp increase in long-term debt, with no visible amortization expense commensurate with the asset base
